title: MagicCommands
description: MagicCommands is designed to provide dynamic command execution using ChatGPT. The plugin allows players to input requests, which are then processed and converted into executable Minecraft commands.
body:
MagicCommands is designed to provide dynamic command execution using ChatGPT. The plugin allows players to input requests via the **_/magic_** command, which are then processed and converted into executable Minecraft commands and ran immediately.

### Examples:

_"Spawn a zombie named Tom holding a shovel"_

_"Give me a strong pickaxe with some cool enchantments"_

_"Summon a spider riding another spider...riding another spider"_

The plugin also makes ChatGPT aware of plugins installed on your server, so as long as there's enough documentation out there for ChatGPT to be familiar with it, it will use commands exclusive to your installed plugins as well!

### Examples:

_"Rollback a radius of 10 blocks by 3 minutes" (Core Protect)_

_"Send a teleport request to Notch" (AdvancedTeleport)_

_"Surround me with a sphere made of glass" (WorldEdit)_

Or even

_"Replace the nearest 10 blocks of sand with dirt. Don't use worldedit!"_

Users can also use **_/magicquestion_** to ask a question about Minecraft.

### Examples:

_"How do I obtain obsidian?"_

_"How do I make a world border?"_


Also, in case you don't want commands to run right away, and you'd like to look at them first and make some adjustments, use _**/magicspell**_

This prints the command in the chat, and lets users click on the command to edit it or run it.


## Warning:
MagicCommands isn't actually magic, and AI is still AI.

It'd be much more accurate to write the command yourself or do some research to find what you're looking for, but if you think ChatGPT can handle it, Magic will handle it just fine.

Expect your magical requests to fail occasionally.

**Errors are more likely to occur on 1.20.3+**

This is mostly just a fun idea I had. If it gains more genuine interest and support, I'll continue to work on it and really round it out.

Please leave feature requests!
